The proposed export of the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile to Vietnam is in its final stages, with only a few clearances remaining, according to BrahMos Aerospace CEO Jaiteerth Joshi.

Key developments:
🔹 Negotiations nearing conclusion
🔹 100th indigenous BrahMos booster delivered
🔹 Indigenous warheads also under development
🔹 Work underway on BrahMos-NG and extended-range variants

If finalized, Vietnam would become one of the largest overseas operators of India's flagship missile system.

tweets.title:
A scramjet cannot operate from a standstill. It first needs a powerful booster to accelerate the vehicle to very high supersonic/hypersonic speeds before the scramjet can ignite.

That is why HSTDV was mounted on an Agni-derived booster, which accelerated the vehicle to the required speed and altitude before the scramjet took over. The same principle is used in other hypersonic programmes worldwide, including Russia’s Zircon, where a rocket booster brings the missile into the scramjet’s operating envelope.

The concept of encapsulating a scramjet vehicle inside a booster and triggering the engine only after the required speed and flight conditions are achieved is not easy to grasp for those new to defence technology.

Image @Kuntal__biswas : Thank you

In a major counter-terror operation, Delhi Police Special Cell has arrested 5 more operatives, taking the total arrests to 8, and dismantling a network allegedly run by Pakistan-based handler Shahzad Bhatti.

According to investigators, members of Tehreek-e-Taliban Hindustan (TTH) were tasked with targeting police personnel, spreading pro-Pakistan propaganda, and promoting the outfit through posters and graffiti across Delhi-NCR.

Arms, ammunition, digital devices, and incriminating material have been seized. Authorities believe the network was part of a larger cross-border terror conspiracy.

The crackdown comes as the NIA conducts raids across Punjab and Haryana to uncover the full extent of the alleged Pakistan-linked terror network.

According to investigators, members of Tehreek-e-Taliban Hindustan (TTH) were tasked with targeting police personnel, spreading pro-Pakistan propaganda, and promoting the outfit through posters and graffiti across Delhi-NCR.

Arms, ammunition, digital devices, and incriminating material have been seized. Authorities believe the network was part of a larger cross-border terror conspiracy.

The crackdown comes as the NIA conducts raids across Punjab and Haryana to uncover the full extent of the alleged Pakistan-linked terror network.
